---
pagetitle: "jstats — Mac setup (build tools, XQuartz)"
title-prefix: ""
---

## Mac setup

A short note on preparing a Mac to install jstats from GitHub. The honest summary
is that **most Mac users need nothing on this page** — jstats installs and runs
cleanly as it is. It's here as a safety net for the rare hitch, and to answer a
question Mac users often arrive with (XQuartz). Once jstats reaches CRAN, none of
it applies.

{{< include _package-basics.qmd >}}

## You probably need nothing extra

jstats is written entirely in R, so installing it doesn't compile anything, and
the install command on the next page (`upgrade = "never"`) tells R not to rebuild
jstats's supporting packages either. On a normal Mac setup, that means jstats
installs with no build tools, no compiler, and no warning. So the two sections
below are *only* for the uncommon case where something goes wrong — you can carry
on to [Step 5 — Install jstats](install-jstats.qmd) now and come back only if you
hit one of these.

## If an install ever asks to compile (build tools)

Very occasionally an install may try to build a supporting package from source and
report a build or compilation error. If that happens, work through these in order
— most people never get past the first step:

1. **Answer "no" to the compile prompt.** If R asks whether to *"install from
   sources the package which needs compilation,"* type `n` and press Enter, then
   run the install again — R will fetch a ready-made version instead. This
   resolves almost every case.
2. **If it still fails**, open the **Terminal** app and run `xcode-select
   --install` to add Apple's command line tools, then **restart R** and retry.
3. **If the error specifically mentions `gfortran`** or a Fortran compiler, the
   community-maintained **macrtools** package installs the full matching toolchain
   for you — see <https://mac.thecoatlessprofessor.com/macrtools/>.

## About XQuartz

You may have heard that R on a Mac "needs XQuartz." For jstats, it does **not**.

**XQuartz** is the X11 windowing system for macOS (Apple stopped bundling X11
years ago). Some R packages rely on it — notably anything built on **Tcl/Tk**
(such as R Commander), the **rgl** package for 3-D graphics, and R's older **X11**
graphics device. jstats uses none of these: its graphics go through the Mac's
native quartz device, so it never touches X11. If you only use jstats, you can
ignore XQuartz entirely.

If you later install a *different* package that does need it, R tells you plainly
when you try to use that feature — you won't be left guessing. At that point,
install XQuartz from <https://www.xquartz.org/> and then restart your Mac (XQuartz
loads at login).

## Next: install jstats

Continue to [Step 5 — Install jstats](install-jstats.qmd). Once jstats moves to
CRAN, this page's safety net falls away — installation becomes a single ready-made
download.
